back_translation: At that time, Jesus went through the grainfield on the Sabbath, and His disciples were hungry, so they began to pick the heads of grain and eat them.
When the Pharisees saw this, they said to Him, "Look, Your disciples are doing what is not lawful on the Sabbath."
He replied to them, "Have you not read what David and those with him did when they were hungry?
How he entered the house of God, and they ate the bread of the Presence, which was not lawful for him or for those with him to eat, except only for the priests?
Or have you not read in the Law that on the Sabbath, the priests in the temple break the Sabbath, but are without guilt?
But I tell you, One greater than the temple is here.
If you had understood the meaning of the saying, 'I desire mercy, not sacrifice,' you would not have condemned the innocent.
Because the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath."
Jesus departed from there and entered their synagogue.
And behold, there was a man with a withered hand. They asked Him, "Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?" so that they might accuse Him.
He replied to them, "Who among you that has one sheep, if that sheep falls into a pit on the Sabbath, will he not grasp it and lift it out?
How much more valuable is a man than a sheep! Therefore, doing good on the Sabbath is lawful."
Then He said to that man, "Stretch out your hand." And he stretched it out, and his hand was restored to normal, healed like the other hand.
But the Pharisees went out and conspired together about Him, how they might kill Him.
When Jesus knew this, He withdrew from that place, and many people followed Him, and He healed them all.
And He warned them strictly not to reveal Him to all people.
This was to fulfill what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying:
"Behold, My Servant, the one I have chosen, the One who is My beloved, the One in whom My soul delights. I will put My Spirit upon Him, and He will proclaim justice to the nations.
He will not quarrel or cry out loudly; no one will hear His voice in the streets.
A bruised reed He will not break, a smoldering wick He will not extinguish, until He leads justice to victory.
And all the nations will place their hope in His name."
Then someone brought a demon-possessed man who was blind and mute to Him, and He healed the man, so that the mute man could both speak and see.
All the crowd were astonished and said to one another, "Could this man be the Son of David?"
But when the Pharisees heard this, they said, "This man does not drive out demons except by Beelzebul, the ruler of demons."
But Jesus knew their thoughts and said to them, "Any kingdom divided against itself will be laid waste, and any city or house divided against itself cannot stand firm.
And if Satan drives out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then can his kingdom stand firm?
And if I drive out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your sons drive them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
But if I drive out demons by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God has come to you already.
Or how can anyone enter the house of the strong man and seize his possessions unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can plunder the strong man's house.
The one who is not on my side is against me, and the one who does not gather with me scatters.
For this reason I tell you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven to people, but blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.
Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man, that word will be forgiven him. But whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit, that word will not be forgiven him, either in this age or in the age to come.
Make a tree good and its fruit also good, or make a tree rotten and its fruit also rotten. For a tree is known by its fruit.
You brood of vipers, since you are evil, how can you speak good things? Because the mouth speaks from what overflows from within the heart.
A good person brings good things out from the good storehouse, and an evil person brings evil things out from the evil storehouse.
But I tell you, for every careless word that people speak, they will have to give an account for that word on the day of judgment.
Because you will be declared righteous by your own words, and you will be condemned by your own words."
Then some of the scribes and Pharisees said to Him, "Teacher, we want to see a sign from you."
But He replied to them, "This evil and unfaithful generation seeks a sign, but no sign will be given to them except the sign of Jonah the prophet.
For just as Jonah was in the belly of the great fish three days and three nights, so will the Son of Man be in the heart of the earth three days and three nights.

The men of Nineveh will rise up at the day of judgment with this generation and will condemn them, because the Ninevites repented when they heard Jonah's preaching, and behold, One greater than Jonah is here.

The Queen of the South will rise up at the day of judgment with this generation and will condemn them, because she came from the ends of the earth to hear Solomon's wisdom, and behold, One greater than Solomon is here.
When an unclean spirit comes out of a person, it wanders through arid places seeking rest, but finds none.
Then it says, 'I will return to my house from which I came out.' When it comes, it finds the house empty, swept, and arranged in order.

Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself, and they enter in and dwell there, and the final condition of that person becomes worse than the first. So will it be with this evil generation."
While He was still speaking to the crowds, behold, His mother and His brothers came and stood outside, desiring to speak with Him.
Someone said to Him, "Look, Your mother and Your brothers are standing outside, desiring to speak with You."
But He replied to the one who spoke to Him, "Who is My mother? And who are My brothers?"
Then He stretched out His hand toward His disciples and said, 'Behold, here are My mother and My brothers.
Because whoever does the will of My Father who is in heaven, that person is My brother and sister and mother."
